title: hierochloe hirta subsp. arctica
intro: Hierochloe hirta subsp. arctica, commonly known as sweet grass, is a perennial grass native to the Arctic regions of North America. It has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antibacterial properties, which make it effective in treating various health issues such as wounds, skin irritations, and respiratory problems. The medicinal properties of sweet grass include its ability to soothe and calm the nervous system, reduce stress and anxiety, and promote relaxation, making it a valuable remedy for improving overall health and well-being. Sweet grass can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or infused oil, and is often used in aromatherapy to promote a sense of calm and tranquility.
